title: Namespaces, Metadata, Labels, Selectors, Ownership, and Deletion · Reconciliation Triage
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective resource identity and lifecycle policy; preserve status and events, isolate the first causal fault in namespaces, metadata, labels, selectors, ownership, and deletion, and retain a regression check. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in resource identity and lifecycle policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal de…
objectives: Distinguish namespaced and cluster-scoped resources, default namespaces, cross-namespace references, quotas, and isolation limits.
Design stable recommended labels and equality or set selectors without accidental overlap, mutable identity, or controller mismatch.
Separate identifying labels from non-identifying annotations and preserve controller, tooling, audit, and rollout metadata ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug K8s Namespace Scope Boundaries
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug namespace contains similarly named objects, copied labels, a dangling owner reference, and a finalizer blocking deletion. Design names, labels, selectors, annotations, ownership, garbage collection, quota boundaries, and deletion evidence. steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Finalizers Deletion Recovery
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-finalizers-deletion-recovery-1lfjvf". Tailor its metadata-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Finalizers Deletion Recovery.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Finalizers Deletion Recovery into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Finalizers Deletion Recovery, keep K8s Finalizers Deletion Recovery connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-finalizers-deletion-recovery-1lfjvf
  - competency: k8s-finalizers-deletion-recovery
    artifact: metadata-policy.yaml
    decision: finalizers deletion recovery
    environment: remote context with short-lived federated workload identity
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: scheduler event eligible domains skew and placement result
    case-key: 1lfjvf
    change: |
      namespace: intake
      labels: app.kubernetes.io/name=status-api
      selector-owner: deployment-status-api
      owner-uid: retained
      deletion: foreground
      finalizer-controller: archive-cleanup
    verify: kubectl get all -n intake --show-labels -o json && kubectl get namespace intake -o json
    failure: Reject namespace-as-sandbox claims, overlapping selectors, name-only owner matching, or blind finalizer removal.
    repair: Separate scope and security, stabilize identity labels, trace owner UIDs and dependents, and identify external cleanup before deletion.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

Design names, labels, selectors, annotations, ownership, garbage collection, quota boundaries, and deletion evidence. Competency probe: Interpret deletionTimestamp and finalizers, identify the responsible controller, preserve external cleanup evidence, and repair stuck deletion safely.

For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Finalizers Deletion Recovery, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: Deleting finalizers immediately is the normal first repair for any terminating resource.
